articles.title: Courts can’t fix MSP, order farm waivers—why SC set aside Himachal HC’s directions to state govt
articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside directions from the Himachal Pradesh High Court to the state government, ruling that policy decisions such as fixing Minimum Support Price (MSP) and framing schemes fall under the executive and legislature's domain, not the judiciary. The top court emphasized that courts cannot overstep into policy-making or executive functions. This decision underscores the constitutional separation of powers and limits judicial intervention in policy matters.
articles.raw_summary: The top court has held that policy decisions such as fixing MSP, framing schemes and setting up commissions fall within the domain of the executive and legislature, and not the judiciary.

articles.title: Allahabad HC quashed FIR filed Against Advocate for Filing a GST Appeal
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court quashed an FIR filed against an advocate for filing a GST appeal on behalf of a client. The Court ruled that performing professional duties, including adopting a legal interpretation in tax matters, does not amount to conspiracy. This decision reinforces the protection of advocates' roles in tax litigation under GST laws.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that an advocate filing GST appeals and adopting a legal interpretation on behalf of a client cannot be prosecuted for conspiracy merely for performing professional duties. articles.title: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah to address speculation on power transition on May 28
articles.summary: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah is expected to address speculation about a power transition in the state government. A Congress Legislative Party meeting, where the likely successor could be named or elected, is scheduled for May 28. The meeting may formalize the next leader of the Congress government in Karnataka. Leadership changes in state governments can impact policy priorities and administrative decisions.
articles.raw_summary: Party sources indicated that the Congress Legislative Party meeting, where the likely successor of Mr. Siddaramaiah could be named or elected, is likely to held on May 28.

articles.title: NGT Faces Criticism for Judicial Overreach Through Expanding Suo Moto Powers
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) is facing criticism for expanding its suo moto powers, which allow it to take up cases without formal petitions. Concerns include inconsistent expert reports, ad hoc committees, and relaxed evidence standards in NGT proceedings. Critics warn that weak scientific scrutiny could undermine the credibility of environmental adjudication in India. The article highlights potential risks to environmental policy and governance due to these judicial practices.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Nagpur ruled that reassessment proceedings against a deceased taxpayer are invalid if the notice was issued to the deceased and not their legal heir. The tribunal held that the tax department's failure to issue notice to the legal heir, despite knowing of the assessee's death, made the assessment void ab initio. This ruling underscores the importance of procedural compliance in tax reassessments and may influence future tax administration practices.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that reassessment proceedings initiated against a deceased person are void ab initio. Since the department had prior knowledge of the death, failure to issue notice to the legal heir made the assessment invalid. The post ITAT Nagpur Quashes Reassessment as Notice Was Issued to Deceased Assessee appeared first on TaxGuru .

This decision impacts tax treatment of carbon credits and deferred tax payments in India.

articles.title: Supreme Court Allows Appointment Of Manipur DGP From Outside Manipur Cadre, Notes Peculiar Circumstance In State
articles.summary: The Supreme Court allowed the Manipur government to appoint a Director General of Police (DGP) from outside the state's cadre, citing the state's peculiar circumstances. articles.title: US Gas Buyers Cancel Cargoes as Iran War Sends Freight Soaring
articles.summary: Some buyers of US liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) have canceled shipments to Asia due to a sharp rise in freight costs triggered by the Middle East conflict. The surge in shipping rates has made US LPG shipments economically unviable for some Asian buyers, including potential Indian importers. This development highlights the broader impact of geopolitical tensions on global energy trade flows.
articles.raw_summary: Some buyers of US liquefied petroleum gas canceled shipments that would typically be bound for Asia following a surge in freight rates sparked by the conflict in the Middle East.

articles.title: Zoho-backed Netrasemi launches first AI chip, begins customer trials
articles.summary: Kerala-based Netrasemi, backed by Zoho, has successfully tested its A2000 AI chip, now production-ready for edge devices. Early trials are underway with customers in surveillance and automotive sectors, targeting a mid-2027 commercial launch. This Indian-designed chip, built on TSMC's 12nm process, is among the first of its kind.

articles.title: Shadow fleet pollution is burdening under-resourced nations
articles.summary: A fleet of aging, poorly regulated fuel tankers—often used to bypass sanctions—is causing significant environmental pollution. These ships, operating outside standard regulations, are primarily impacting poorer countries with limited resources to address the damage. The article highlights the broader issue of how global maritime loopholes contribute to environmental harm in vulnerable nations.
articles.raw_summary: Decrepit, sanction-busting fuel tankers are flouting regulations and creating environmental consequences for poorer countries The post Shadow fleet pollution is burdening under-resourced nations appeared first on Dialogue Earth .

articles.title: Government operationalises amended insolvency law, sets 14-day admission deadline for NCLT
articles.summary: On May 26, the Indian government implemented amendments to the insolvency law to speed up bankruptcy resolution for companies. The new rules prioritize lenders in cash distribution and set a 14-day deadline for the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) to admit insolvency cases. This aims to streamline financial recovery processes for creditors.
articles.raw_summary: On May 26, significant updates to the insolvency law took effect, geared towards hastening the resolution of companies facing bankruptcy. The new regulations not only bring forth novel ideas but also refine existing processes. Lenders will now have precedence when it comes to cash distribution, streamlining the financial recovery.

articles.title: HC Upholds ITAT Remand for Fresh Cash Flow Verification in Share Premium Addition u/s 68; Human Probability Test Not Enough
articles.summary: The High Court upheld the ITAT's decision to remand a case for fresh verification of cash flows in a share premium addition under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act. The case involved investor companies bringing in large investments while promoters retained majority control despite investing less proportionately. The ruling emphasizes that human probability tests alone are insufficient for such tax assessments. The decision impacts tax compliance and corporate governance practices in India.
articles.raw_summary: AO observed an imbalance: the investor companies brought in massive investments but received a minimal percentage of equity, while the promoters retained overwhelming majority control despite investing a lower proportionate amount (Rs. 45 crores) The post HC Upholds ITAT Remand for Fresh Cash Flow Verification in Share Premium Addition u/s 68; Human Probability Test Not Enough appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Supreme Court Rejects Challenge To S.63(4) BSA Mandating Hash Value Disclosure For Electronic Evidence
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a challenge to Section 63(4) of the Bharatiya Sakshya Act (BSA), which requires disclosure of hash values for electronic evidence. The Court clarified that a Madras High Court ruling, which stated that only government-notified experts can issue certificates under this section, is not a binding precedent. This decision impacts how electronic evidence is handled in legal proceedings in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Court however held that the Madras HC ruling that only Govt-notified experts can give a certificate under S.63(4) is not a binding precedent.

articles.title: Think it's hot now? The next five years will smash records: UN

The findings could influence India's climate policies, renewable energy strategies, and international negotiations on emissions reduction.

articles.title: Emotional scenes as Abdul Rahim arrives home in Kerala after serving 20-year jail term in Saudi
articles.summary: Abdul Rahim, a native of Kerala's Kozhikode district, returned home after serving a 20-year jail term in Saudi Arabia. He was initially sentenced to death in 2006 for the murder of his employer's 14-year-old differently abled son. The case raises questions about consular access for Indian prisoners abroad and legal protections for vulnerable groups, including children with disabilities.
articles.raw_summary: A native of Kodampuzha in Kozhikode district of Kerala, Abdul Rahim was imprisoned and sentenced to death in 2006 for the murder of 14-year-old differently abled son of his employer. His jail term ended on May 19

articles.title: Jamiat chief says do not sacrifice prohibited animals, avoid sharing slaughter photos on social media

The suggestions reflect demands for policy action to balance religious practices with social harmony and governance.

articles.summary: Doctors in rural Punjab are improving heart attack care by using WhatsApp for coordination, training frontline healthcare workers, and administering the clot-busting injection tenecteplase. This initiative aims to bridge gaps in timely treatment for heart attack patients in remote areas. The approach combines digital tools, workforce training, and medical interventions to enhance emergency cardiac care.
articles.raw_summary: By combining WhatsApp-based coordination, training for frontline healthcare workers, and the clot-busting injection tenecteplase, doctors are improving access to timely heart attack care in rural Punjab

articles.title: To vote is a ‘valuable constitutional right’ but not absolute: Supreme Court in SIR verdict
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the right to vote is a valuable constitutional right but not absolute, emphasizing the need for accurate electoral rolls to maintain election integrity. The court highlighted the link between election integrity and the correctness of electoral rolls in its verdict on the SIR case. This ruling has significant implications for India's electoral governance and legal framework.
articles.raw_summary: The integrity of elections is inextricably linked to the correctness of the electoral roll, the top court said.

articles.title: ED Raids on Vijayan’s Residences ‘Politically Motivated’: CPI(M) Gen Secy

The party also highlighted Vijayan's past arrest and alleged police brutality during the Emergency period when he was an MLA. The CPI(M) demanded an explanation for the raids, framing them as an abuse of power by central agencies.

articles.title: Why are Europe's biggest defense projects in trouble?
articles.summary: Europe's €100 billion fighter jet project is facing fractures due to leadership disputes, conflicting military priorities, and industrial control issues. Tensions have now extended to a Franco-German tank project. These challenges could impact defense collaborations and supply chains, raising concerns about project delays and strategic alignment among European nations.

articles.title: States under fiscal strain amid high welfare spend: Morgan Stanley
articles.summary: Indian states are facing financial strain due to slower revenue growth and high welfare expenditures, leading to elevated fiscal deficits. Morgan Stanley projects state fiscal deficits to remain at 3.2% of Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) through FY27, while the combined fiscal deficit is expected to rise to 7.9%. This fiscal pressure may necessitate policy adjustments or reforms to manage spending and revenue.
articles.raw_summary: States are facing fiscal pressure as revenue growth slows and welfare spending remains high, leading to elevated deficits. Morgan Stanley reports state fiscal deficits are projected to stay at 3.2% of GSDP through FY27, while the consolidated fiscal deficit is expected to rise to 7.9%.

articles.summary: Vellore Corporation officials demolished illegal stalls and encroachments at a newly constructed bus terminus. The demolition drive was carried out by the local government authority to address public safety concerns.

articles.title: TDP resolves to implement 33% women’s quota in party tickets in next elections
articles.summary: The Telugu Desam Party (TDP) announced a resolution to reserve 33% of party tickets for women in the next elections. This decision was made during the party's annual conclave, Mahanadu, held in Mangalagiri, Guntur district. The move aims to increase women's representation in politics and aligns with broader discussions on gender equality and women's rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: Lokesh made the announcement on the opening day of Mahanadu, the two-day annual conclave of the TDP being held at the party headquarters in Mangalagiri town of Guntur district.

articles.title: Retrospective VAT Registration Cancellation Does Not Automatically Make Purchases Bogus: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Ahmedabad Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the cost of improvement cannot be disallowed solely because suppliers' VAT registrations were later cancelled retrospectively. The tribunal sent the matter back for verification of purchase and transportation documents. This decision impacts how businesses handle tax compliance and disputes related to retrospective cancellations of supplier registrations.

Discussions also focused on strengthening bilateral investment and technology partnerships between the two nations.

articles.title: Madras HC Refuses Injunction as Shareholder Validly Requisitioned EGM
articles.summary: The Madras High Court refused to grant an injunction after a shareholder, holding sufficient voting power, validly requisitioned an Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) under Section 169 of the Companies Act. The court upheld the shareholder's statutory right to call the EGM if the board fails to act. This ruling reinforces corporate governance norms and clarifies legal recourse for shareholders in disputes with company boards.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that a shareholder holding requisite voting strength has a statutory right under Section 169 to convene an extraordinary general meeting if the board fails to act. The post Madras HC Refuses Injunction as Shareholder Validly Requisitioned EGM appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Piyush Goyal wraps up Canada visit focusing on India-Canada CEPA talks, $50 billion trade goal
articles.summary: Union Minister Piyush Goyal concluded a three-day visit to Canada. The trip focused on boosting trade and investment between India and Canada. Discussions also centered on the ongoing Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement negotiations. The visit reinforced strong economic ties and highlighted India as a prime investment destination. articles.title: India needs structural reforms, not subsidies, to shield economy from West Asia shocks: Report
articles.summary: India must shift from subsidy reliance during global crises to structural reforms for economic resilience. Rising geopolitical tensions highlight vulnerabilities in energy, industrial feedstocks, and supply chains. A white paper by Think Change Forum proposes a three-point agenda focusing on export competitiveness, import discipline, and trade defence. Key recommendations include correcting inverted duty structures and bringing natural gas under GST.
articles.raw_summary: India must shift from subsidy reliance during global crises to structural reforms for economic resilience. Rising geopolitical tensions highlight vulnerabilities in energy, industrial feedstocks, and supply chains. A white paper by Think Change Forum proposes a three-point agenda focusing on export competitiveness, import discipline, and trade defence. Key recommendations include correcting inverted duty structures and bringing natural gas under GST.

The agreement aims to protect Taiwan's semiconductor industry from trade restrictions. This development is significant for global semiconductor supply chains, including India's push for domestic chip manufacturing.

articles.title: Bangladesh: Switch from paddy to fruit crops leaves poor farmers and workers struggling
articles.summary: In Bangladesh's Barind Tract, farmers are shifting from water-intensive paddy crops to fruit farming due to a worsening water crisis. However, this transition is only feasible for wealthier farmers, leaving poorer farmers and agricultural workers struggling. The shift raises concerns about food security, rural livelihoods, and equitable access to resources. The article underscores the broader challenges of climate-induced agricultural adaptation.
articles.raw_summary: In the north-western Barind Tract, a water crisis is driving the transition, but only among those who can afford it.

articles.title: Singapore court orders jail term for Byju Raveendran for contempt of court
articles.summary: A Singapore court has sentenced Byju Raveendran, founder of BYJU’S, to six months in jail for contempt of court. The court found he disobeyed multiple orders related to his assets and directed him to surrender to authorities and pay costs of S$90,000. This ruling highlights issues of corporate accountability and legal compliance in cross-border contexts.

articles.title: The new fault line in Manipur—inside growing Naga-Kuki conflict & why it’s worrying security agencies
articles.summary: Manipur is experiencing escalating clashes between Naga and Kuki tribal groups, adding to existing Meitei-Kuki violence. The conflict is raising fresh security concerns for agencies and could have broader implications for tribal rights and governance in the state. The violence reflects deep-seated ethnic tensions that may require policy attention or intervention from authorities.
articles.raw_summary: After three years of Meitei-Kuki violence, rising clashes between Nagas and Kukis are reshaping Manipur’s conflict and triggering fresh security concerns.

articles.title: Bharti Airtel defends 'Priority Postpaid' service before DoT panel, denies net neutrality violations
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel defended its 'Priority Postpaid' service before a Department of Telecommunications (DoT) panel, denying allegations of net neutrality violations. The company argued that restricting mainstream 5G features could hinder the potential of future 6G technologies in India. The DoT panel is reviewing the service's compliance with telecom regulations.
articles.raw_summary: Bharti Airtel said not allowing the use of mainstream features of 5G to offer services will jeopardise the potential of 6G in the country.

articles.title: In Pakistan too, ethnic Bengalis face challenges with citizenship papers
articles.summary: In Pakistan, many ethnic Bengalis have been incorrectly classified as foreigners, denying them access to education, healthcare, employment, and marriage opportunities. This issue highlights broader challenges faced by minority groups in securing citizenship rights. While the article focuses on Pakistan, it may influence discussions on similar rights violations in India, particularly concerning refugee and minority policies.

articles.title: n-Hexane Not ‘Motor Spirit’ Without Proof of Engine Use Suitability: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that n-Hexane cannot be classified as 'motor spirit' under Chapter 27 of India's tax regulations unless it is proven suitable for use in spark ignition engines. The Revenue Department failed to provide evidence that the imported n-Hexane met this criterion. The decision impacts import classifications and tax liabilities for such products. The ruling was delivered in response to a legal dispute over customs duty classification.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that n-Hexane could not be classified as motor spirit under Chapter 27 merely because its flash point was below 25°C. The Revenue failed to prove that the imported product was suitable for use in spark ignition engines. The post n-Hexane Not ‘Motor Spirit’ Without Proof of Engine Use Suitability: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Bangalore ITAT held that uncorroborated WhatsApp chats and retracted statements are insufficient to sustain large on-money additions in search assessments. Additions based purely on estimates without incriminating evidence were deleted. articles.title: Predator-prey relationships and habitat quality shape a tiger reserve
articles.summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. After reintroduction, a study found that prey availability and habitat quality are crucial for tiger population recovery. The findings underscore the need for balanced conservation strategies beyond just reintroducing carnivores. The research was published by Mongabay India.
articles.raw_summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. They were reintroduced a few years later and have since re-established populations. While carnivore reintroduction has been considered as an important strategy to restore ecological balance, a recent study highlights that prey abundance and habitat quality also play an essential role. articles.title: Game of skills is gambling if players put money on ‘uncertain outcomes’: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that even games requiring skill can be classified as gambling under India's GST law if players wager money on uncertain outcomes. The court also clarified that online gambling proceeds are taxable. This decision has significant implications for the regulation and taxation of skill-based games in India.
articles.raw_summary: The court said even if a game requires skill, it becomes gambling under the GST law when players risk their money on uncertain outcomes and laid out how online gambling money is taxable. The post Game of skills is gambling if players put money on ‘uncertain outcomes’: SC appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

This initiative aims to foster innovation and help startups scale globally, aligning with the state's economic vision.

This move aims to foster innovation and global scaling for startups in key sectors aligned with the state's economic vision.

articles.title: ET Graphics | Base to basket: India's IIP gets a major makeover
articles.summary: A new Index of Industrial Production launches on June 1. The base year shifts to 2022-23. Coverage expands to include minor minerals, rare earths, and waste management. This appointment reflects a state-level political and governance decision with potential implications for livestock policy and administration in Tamil Nadu.

articles.title: PIL seeks CBI probe into Cockroach Janta Party activities, alleges misuse of court remarks
articles.summary: A public interest litigation (PIL) has been filed in the Supreme Court requesting a CBI investigation into the activities of the 'Cockroach Janta Party'. The plea alleges misuse of the apex court's remarks for commercial exploitation and highlights concerns over fake advocates with fraudulent degrees. It also warns against turning constitutional proceedings into publicity stunts for digital campaigns.

articles.title: No girl should give up education over lack of toilets or sanitary pads: SC tells Centre
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (Justices J B Pardiwala and R Mahadevan) directed the central government to ensure that girls are not compelled to leave school because of inadequate sanitation facilities like toilets or sanitary pads. The bench emphasized that schools must provide safe and hygienic conditions to prevent girls from being pushed into domestic responsibilities. This ruling addresses a critical barrier to girls' education and highlights the state's obligation to uphold their right to schooling.
articles.raw_summary: A Bench of Justices J B Pardiwala and R Mahadevan observed that girls should not be pushed into domestic responsibilities or forced to stay at home simply because schools fail to provide safe sanitation facilities.

articles.title: Fed's Cook says she is prepared to raise rates if inflation doesn't ease
articles.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook stated she is prepared to raise U.S. interest rates if inflation does not ease, citing rising energy costs and wage pressures. She acknowledged mixed economic risks from tariffs, the Iran war, and AI investment but noted the labor market remains stable. The Fed currently sees no immediate need for rate cuts.
articles.raw_summary: Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook signaled support for keeping US interest rates steady, citing mixed economic risks from tariffs, the Iran war, and rising AI investment. She warned inflation is trending higher due to energy costs and wage pressures but said the labor market remains stable with no immediate need for cuts.

articles.title: Telangana firm Coempt Edutech in focus amid CBSE-OSM row

Congress MP Rahul Gandhi recently criticized the CBSE over complaints related to OSM, raising questions about the regulation and oversight of such educational technology platforms in India. The issue highlights concerns about data privacy, accountability, and the role of private firms in public education systems.

Investors are awaiting key U.S. economic data for policy signals. Other precious metals like silver, platinum, and palladium also declined amid the uncertainty.

Investors are now keenly awaiting crucial U.S. economic data for further policy direction. Meanwhile, other precious metals like silver, platinum, and palladium also experienced declines.

articles.title: Jingle bell blues: Christmas orders from US, Europe drop 10-15% as West Asia crisis bites
articles.summary: Christmas orders from the US and Europe for goods like decorations and gifts have dropped by 10-15% this year. Exporters attribute the decline to global economic uncertainties, higher raw material prices, and increased shipping costs. The West Asia crisis has further exacerbated the situation. Exporters remain optimistic about a potential surge in orders during June, a key period for holiday season bookings.

articles.title: Copper hits one-week low as Middle East escalation clouds growth outlook

This development could impact global semiconductor supply chains and India's push for domestic chip manufacturing under initiatives like the Semiconductor Mission.

articles.summary: Institutional investors showed strong interest in Coal India's offer for sale on Wednesday. They placed bids worth approximately Rs 19,000 crore. This demand significantly exceeded the portion reserved for them. The government is selling up to a 2 percent stake in the company. Retail investors can bid on May 29.

articles.title: Ugandan woman, suspected of Ebola infection, tests negative in Karnataka
articles.summary: A Ugandan woman suspected of Ebola infection in Karnataka tested negative, but the state's health department continues to monitor the situation closely. Karnataka's health minister stated that authorities remain fully prepared to take necessary measures. The incident underscores ongoing vigilance in public health preparedness.
articles.raw_summary: Karnataka minister says health department continues to closely monitor the situation and remains fully prepared to take all necessary measures

articles.title: Eid prayers disallowed at Srinagar's Jama Masjid for 8th year in a row
articles.summary: For the eighth consecutive year, Muslims in Srinagar are barred from performing Eid prayers at the historic Jama Masjid and Eidgah. The restriction, enforced by authorities, has drawn criticism over concerns about religious freedom and minority rights in the region. The recurring nature of the ban raises questions about the underlying policies or administrative decisions governing such restrictions.
articles.raw_summary: Muslims in Kashmir are not allowed to perform Eid prayers at the Eidgah and the historic Jama Masjid for the eighth consecutive year.

articles.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham questions Pakistan’s role as US-Iran mediator
articles.summary: US Senator Lindsey Graham questioned Pakistan's suitability as a mediator in US-Iran talks, calling it 'more than problematic' due to Islamabad's historical hostility toward Israel. The remarks underscore concerns about Pakistan's role in regional diplomacy and its potential impact on India's strategic interests, given the country's geopolitical dynamics.
articles.raw_summary: Graham said Pakistan as a mediator was more than problematic and Islamabad’s animosity towards Israel was long standing

articles.title: ‘Oman will behave or we’ll have to blow them up': Trump's big warning on Strait of Hormuz control
articles.summary: Donald Trump warned that Oman must comply with U.S. demands regarding control of the Strait of Hormuz, or face military action. The remark came in response to a proposed arrangement where Iran and Oman would share control of the strategic waterway. The Strait is a vital route for global oil shipments, including India's crude oil imports, making this a matter of significant geopolitical and energy security concern.
articles.raw_summary: Donald Trump made the remark after being asked whether he would accept a proposed arrangement under which Iran and Oman would share control of Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: ‘Congress encouraged ED’: Big CPI(M) claim after Vijayan's home raided; Tharoor has a counter
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted searches at around 10 locations across Kerala, including the rented residence of a state political leader. The CPI(M) alleged that the Congress party encouraged the ED's actions, while Congress leader Tharoor countered the claim. The raids have sparked political debate over the use of investigative agencies in state politics.

articles.title: In post-Naxal Dantewada, survey begins for Bodhghat dam, raising displacement fears
articles.summary: A survey has begun for the Bodhghat Multipurpose Project, a long-planned dam in Bastar's Dantewada district first proposed in 1979. The project could displace local communities, raising concerns about tribal rights and resettlement. The survey follows the post-Naxal conflict period in the region. The dam is one of India's oldest pending river valley projects.
articles.raw_summary: The Bodhghat Multipurpose Project is one of the oldest pending river valley projects in Bastar, first envisaged in 1979.

articles.title: CBSE test website had 'master password' that could be used to tamper marks, claims 'hacker' Nisarga Adhikary
articles.summary: A teenager named Nisarga Adhikary claimed that the CBSE's testing website had a 'master password' that could allow tampering with marks. CBSE denied that the actual evaluation portal was compromised, stating the vulnerabilities were only on a testing site. The incident raises concerns about data security and governance in educational institutions.
articles.raw_summary: CBSE has denied that the actual evaluation portal was compromised, saying the vulnerabilities highlighted by the teenager related only to a “testing site”.

articles.title: Unusual summer pollution spike highlights Aravallis’ diminished role as dust barrier
articles.summary: A recent spike in summer pollution has drawn attention to the deteriorating condition of the Aravalli ranges, which historically acted as a natural barrier against dust. The article underscores how the destruction of these hills may be contributing to worsening air quality. Environmental experts and activists have long warned about the ecological impact of Aravalli degradation.
articles.raw_summary: The spike in pollution brings back into focus the destruction of the Aravalli ranges, which for centuries acted as a natural barrier.

articles.title: AIADMK factions call truce, withdraw disqualification pleas

articles.title: 300 booked after attack on ED officials following raid linked to former Kerala CM Vijayan
articles.summary: Around 300 people were booked after stones and bricks were allegedly thrown at vehicles carrying Enforcement Directorate (ED) officials during a raid in Kerala. The attack seriously injured the driver of one of the cars. The raid is reportedly linked to a case involving former Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan. The incident raises concerns about law enforcement operations and public safety.

articles.title: EAM Jaishankar, Ukrainian counterpart exchange views on war and bilateral ties in Cyprus

The discussions focused on the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war and bilateral relations between India and Ukraine. Jaishankar was invited by the EU to attend the forum, highlighting India's engagement in international diplomatic dialogues.
